 {"id":"bd-6a6a","title":"Preserve canonical project label casing in label suggestions","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-27T20:19:51.476368364Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T20:23:27.271667680Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T20:23:27.271381088Z","close_reason":"Label suggestions now preserve canonical project label casing while matching case-insensitively.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-6d9n","title":"Improve export_sqlite.rs unit test coverage","description":"export_sqlite.rs has 1577 lines of production code but only 8 unit tests. Add comprehensive unit tests covering edge cases, error handling, and key export paths.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T02:59:58.187325218Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T03:04:04.874663821Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T03:04:04.872987573Z","close_reason":"Added 18 new unit tests to export_sqlite.rs covering: collect_dependency_rows (filtering, dedup, sorting, empty targets), collect_comment_rows (composite IDs, cross-issue sorting), normalized_source_repo (empty/whitespace/normal), emit_bootstrap_config error path, empty issue population, idempotent population, title handling (none/blank), hash_file consistency, path helpers, and chunk reassembly. Total tests: 8→26. All 1,335 tests pass with clean clippy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-6gv","title":"Config system: per-metric toggles, timeouts, size-based thresholds","description":"WHAT: Implement AnalysisConfig struct with per-metric enable/disable toggles (e.g. enable_pagerank, enable_betweenness, enable_hits, enable_eigenvector, enable_kcore), computation timeouts per metric, and size-based thresholds (e.g. skip betweenness for graphs >10k nodes). WHY: Go's analysis engine has configurable analysis via AnalysisOptions struct (pkg/analysis/analysis.go) that lets users tune which metrics run and their resource bounds. Our Rust engine currently hardcodes all metrics as always-on with no timeouts. Without this, large graphs can hang or OOM. IMPLEMENTATION: Add AnalysisConfig to src/analysis/graph.rs, wire it through GraphAnalyzer::new(), expose via --analysis-config CLI flag (YAML/JSON file) and BV_ANALYSIS_CONFIG env var. Default config should match current behavior (all enabled, generous timeouts). ACCEPTANCE: Config file loads, metrics can be individually disabled, timeouts abort long-running computations gracefully, robot output includes which metrics were skipped and why. PARENT: bd-3in","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:02:38.038722666Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-08T23:11:17.452521398Z","closed_at":"2026-03-08T23:11:17.452107073Z","close_reason":"Implemented AnalysisConfig with per-metric enable/disable toggles and size-based thresholds. Added SkippedMetric tracking, compute_metrics_with_config(), factory methods (full/for_size/triage_only), serde support, and Analyzer::new_with_config(). 9 new unit tests. All 669 tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":42,"issue_id":"bd-6gv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"DESIGN: AnalysisConfig should be a simple YAML/JSON file: {metrics: {pagerank: {enabled: true, max_iterations: 100}, betweenness: {enabled: true, timeout_ms: 5000, max_nodes: 10000}, ...}, cache: {ttl_seconds: 300}, async: {background_threshold: 1000}}. Load from file path, env var, or CLI flag. Merge with defaults (all enabled, generous limits). Serialize back to JSON for robot output echo.","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:09:13Z"},{"id":57,"issue_id":"bd-6gv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"ADDITIONAL DETAIL from Go config.go: The AnalysisConfig has per-metric fields: ComputeBetweenness (with BetweennessMode: exact/approximate/skip), ComputePageRank, ComputeHITS, ComputeCycles, ComputeEigenvector, ComputeCriticalPath, ComputeKCore, ComputeArticulation, ComputeSlack. Each has associated timeout fields and skip reasons. Go provides factory functions: DefaultConfig() (all enabled, exact betweenness), ConfigForSize(nodeCount, edgeCount int) (adaptive), FullAnalysisConfig(), TriageConfig() (PageRank+Betweenness only), NoPhase2Config(). Env overrides: BV_SKIP_PHASE2, BV_PHASE2_TIMEOUT_S via ApplyEnvOverrides(). Rust should implement all factory functions.","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:33:42Z"},{"id":95,"issue_id":"bd-6gv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"TESTING REQUIREMENT: This task is NOT complete until it includes inline unit tests. Config: test load/merge/default. Cache: test hit/miss/TTL/invalidation. Feedback: test round-trip/apply/corrupt-file. Async: test fast-return/eventual-complete.","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:37:47Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-6u5b","title":"Fix multi-repo workspace git history correlation","description":"build_robot_history_output currently resolves a single git root and runs load_git_commits once even when the load target is a multi-repo workspace. That means --robot-history can miss or misattribute commits for workspace repos outside the resolved root. Implement per-repo git history loading/correlation for workspace mode and add regression coverage proving each repo contributes its own commits.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-05T23:44:05.762939735Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-05T23:56:49.793131605Z","closed_at":"2026-04-05T23:56:49.792739492Z","close_reason":"Fixed workspace --robot-history git correlation to load commits per repo and added multi-repo regression coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-7oo","title":"Close remaining legacy parity gaps vs beads_viewer Go","description":"Background:
The Rust port already covers most robot-mode and baseline TUI behavior, but the parity audit found that full legacy replacement is still blocked by a concentrated set of gaps: static export/pages fidelity, preview/watch/pages UX, workspace default-discovery behavior, richer history search modes, and several high-touch TUI interaction surfaces. This epic is now the canonical source of truth for finishing that work.

Goal:
Finish the remaining legacy parity work in a way that is implementation-ready for future agents without requiring them to reread the historical markdown planning docs or reconstruct intent from old sessions.

Why this matters:
The project goal is not just partial feature coverage; it is a trustworthy Rust replacement for the legacy Go `bv` binary. Until the remaining surfaces below are closed and re-verified, the project cannot honestly claim complete functional parity.","design":"Execution model: Treat this bead as the umbrella parity epic and planning container. Future agents should usually claim leaf tasks, not this container, unless they are actively curating the plan itself. Keep the remaining work split into self-contained feature tracks: export or artifacts, preview/watch/pages UX, workspace/history parity, TUI parity, and docs or architecture alignment. Require an explicit verification ladder for every completed track: fast unit or state tests for pure logic, integration or conformance tests for IO contracts, shell-level e2e scripts for operator journeys, and a final `rch`-routed sweep. Use explicit dependencies to encode both technical sequencing and practical merge-risk constraints when multiple tasks touch the same Rust modules. Every implementation bead must carry its own validation burden; do not defer evidence gathering to a final cleanup pass.

Validation policy: All cargo builds, clippy runs, and test runs must continue to go through `rch`. E2E scripts and parity harnesses must preserve detailed step logs, artifact paths, and focused failure summaries so regressions are diagnosable without ad hoc reproduction. Update parity docs only after the underlying behavior and all required evidence layers are green.","acceptance_criteria":"- All child feature tracks and their leaf tasks are completed.
- `FEATURE_PARITY.md` and `README.md` reflect reality instead of stale claims.
- Export/pages, workspace/history, and TUI parity gaps called out in the audit are either implemented or explicitly de-scoped with written rationale.
- Each user-facing track is backed by unit, integration, and shell-e2e evidence with detailed failure logging.
- Final parity verification passes through `rch` with clean `cargo check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, and the relevant lib, integration, conformance, and e2e suites.","notes":"Current audit snapshot to preserve for future agents: Rust now has baseline pages export, export hooks for markdown and pages, workspace-aware watch polling, preview status endpoint support, and browser-open support. Remaining major export/pages gap: legacy Go still ships a richer SQLite-backed static export with bundled viewer assets, offline/CSP behavior, deployment artifacts, and a real interactive pages wizard. Remaining workspace/history gap: explicit workspace config support exists, but Go-style workspace discovery/default behavior and history search modes are still behind. Remaining TUI gap: board, insights, graph, and main-detail surfaces are functional but still less rich than the legacy Go UI. Documentation gap: some old parity rows overclaimed implemented behavior or lumped already-ported work into stale `planned` buckets.

Planning rule: The feature and epic beads in this subtree are coordination containers. Execution priority should usually live on their leaf tasks so `bv` recommendations stay actionable for implementers. Keep these container beads low-priority and normally `open`, not `in_progress`, unless someone is actively curating the plan itself.","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-07T23:10:39.284488039Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T12:09:40.243746169Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T12:09:40.239965012Z","close_reason":"Umbrella epic complete. All child features closed: TUI parity (bd-7oo.4), export parity (bd-7oo.1/2), workspace/history parity (bd-7oo.3), docs finalized (bd-7oo.5), final sweep passed (bd-7oo.6). 1,293 tests, bvr confirmed as functional legacy replacement.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["legacy-port","meta","parity"]}
 {"id":"bd-7oo.1","title":"Complete static export artifact parity with legacy bv","description":"Background:
The current Rust `--export-pages` surface is a baseline JSON/HTML bundle. The legacy Go export is materially richer: SQLite-backed data model, bundled viewer assets, offline/static-host readiness, and additional generated artifacts used by GitHub Pages and other deployment flows.

Goal:
Make Rust static export a functional replacement for the legacy export artifact contract instead of a reduced baseline.","design":"Approach:
- Freeze the artifact contract from legacy tests first so implementation is driven by executable expectations instead of memory.
- Land backend/export semantics before polishing wizard/deploy UX.
- Keep this feature focused on what `--export-pages` writes to disk; preview/watch/wizard behavior lives in a sibling feature track.
- Treat this track as complete only when it has the full verification ladder: reusable contract fixtures, code-level unit or integration evidence, and shell e2e proof with retained artifact logs.
- Prefer a discoverable operator workflow: parity scripts should plug into an existing e2e entrypoint or a clearly documented parity runner so future maintainers do not have to remember bespoke commands.","acceptance_criteria":"- Child tasks under this feature are completed.
- `--export-pages` writes the artifact set needed to stand in for the legacy Go export surface.
- The resulting behavior is backed by executable contract fixtures, integration or conformance tests, and shell e2e evidence with detailed logging rather than doc assertions alone.
- A future maintainer can rerun the export proof set from a documented entrypoint and understand failures from the retained logs and artifact inventory.","notes":"Audit context:
Legacy Go currently writes `beads.sqlite3`, `beads.sqlite3.config.json`, richer JSON payloads, viewer assets, and deployment-friendly extras. The Rust port currently emits a much smaller bundle and therefore still understates its remaining parity gap.","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":480,"created_at":"2026-03-07T23:39:27.968597649Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T21:56:54.229649667Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T21:56:54.229254627Z","close_reason":"All 7 children complete: artifact contract frozen, SQLite backend ported (schema + population + config + JSON sidecars), viewer asset pack ported (packaging + static-host refs + CSP/cache)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["export-pages","legacy-port","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-7oo.1","depends_on_id":"bd-7oo","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T23:39:27.968597649Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-7oo.1.1","title":"Freeze the canonical legacy export artifact contract in Rust tests","description":"Background:
Legacy export behavior is currently spread across many Go e2e tests. If future agents implement export parity from memory, they will keep missing edge cases and artifact details.

Goal:
Translate the legacy export artifact inventory and expectations into Rust-side tests or fixture assertions so the contract is local, executable, and self-documenting.","design":"Implementation expectations:
- Mine the legacy export, offline, incremental, and cloudflare tests for concrete artifact expectations.
- Capture required files, metadata shapes, and behavioral invariants directly in Rust tests or compact fixture helpers.
- Use this task to settle naming and required/optional artifact categories before larger export refactors begin.
- Record provenance for each contract item so future agents can tell which legacy test or behavior it came from and why it matters.
- Classify each artifact or invariant as must-have parity, explicitly deferred, or non-goal, and make test failures name the category and missing contract item precisely.
- Shape the helpers so later integration tests and shell e2e scripts can reuse the same contract vocabulary instead of inventing parallel checklists.","acceptance_criteria":"- Rust test helpers describe the required export artifact inventory and major invariants.
- Future export tasks can point to Rust tests instead of rereading legacy Go test suites.
- The artifact contract clearly distinguishes must-have parity items from intentionally deferred or non-goal items.
- Contract fixtures or helpers preserve legacy provenance and produce precise failure messages that identify which artifact, invariant, or contract class regressed.
- Later export verification beads can reuse this contract directly rather than restating it in ad hoc assertions.","notes":"This task is deliberately front-loaded to avoid repeated debate about whether a missing file or payload is a true parity gap. It should encode the audit findings into testable local truth.

Future-self guidance: if a later export decision feels ambiguous, the answer should come from the contract produced here. If the contract seems incomplete, extend it with provenance and rationale before implementing more export behavior.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-07T23:41:50.938259063Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-08T22:16:42.654829322Z","closed_at":"2026-03-08T22:16:42.654811388Z","close_reason":"Encoded legacy export contract in Rust tests with provenance/status matrix; validated via rch in isolated clean worktree (fmt/check/clippy + contract tests).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["export-pages","legacy-port","parity","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-7oo.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-7oo.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T23:41:50.938259063Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
